title: PIR Auto Light
subtitle: Smart Motion Sensor Lighting System
overview: This session introduces students to PIR motion sensors and automatic lighting systems. Students will learn how motion detection works and build a smart auto-light project using Arduino and PIR sensors.

images._note: Add photos from the actual classroom session — PIR sensor, Arduino setup, student activity, auto light demo, wiring diagrams.

Jumper wires connect all electronic parts together

keyTopics: What is a PIR Sensor? — Motion detector using body heat
Automation — Devices working automatically without touching
Infrared Detection — PIR senses heat movement
Arduino UNO — Smart Brain controlling the system
LED Light — Turns ON when motion is detected
Digital Input Signals — Sensor sends HIGH or LOW signal
Smart Home Technology — Automatic lights in buildings
Energy Saving — Lights turn OFF when no movement

activity.title: Build a PIR Motion Sensor Auto Light
steps: Connect PIR sensor VCC to Arduino 5V
Connect PIR OUT pin to Arduino digital pin
Connect LED with resistor on breadboard
Upload Arduino PIR auto light code
Wave hand in front of sensor
Wait a few seconds — LED turns OFF automatically

quiz.explanation: PIR stands for Passive Infrared Sensor. It detects heat and motion from humans and animals.

quiz.q: What does a PIR sensor detect?
quiz.explanation: PIR sensors detect infrared heat energy from moving humans or animals.

quiz.q: What happens when motion is detected?
quiz.explanation: When motion is detected, the PIR sensor sends a signal and the LED turns ON.

quiz.q: Which component acts as the smart brain?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO controls the sensor and light system like a smart brain.

quiz.q: Why are PIR auto lights useful?
quiz.explanation: Auto lights save electricity because they turn ON only when needed.

quiz.q: Which signal does the PIR sensor send when motion is detected?
quiz.explanation: The PIR sensor sends a HIGH digital signal when motion is detected.

quiz.q: What is used to connect electronic parts without soldering?
quiz.explanation: Breadboards allow students to build circuits easily without soldering.

quiz.q: What type of light is commonly used in Arduino projects?
quiz.explanation: LEDs are small, safe, and energy-efficient lights used in projects.

quiz.q: Where are automatic motion lights commonly used?
quiz.explanation: Automatic lights are used in staircases, malls, washrooms, and smart homes.

quiz.q: What happens when no motion is detected for some time?
quiz.explanation: The auto light turns OFF automatically when no movement is detected.

quiz.q: Which pin on the PIR sensor sends data to Arduino?
quiz.explanation: The OUT pin sends motion detection signals to the Arduino.

quiz.q: What kind of project is a PIR auto light?
quiz.explanation: A PIR auto light is an automation project because it works automatically.

quiz.q: What powers the Arduino UNO during class activity?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO is commonly powered using a USB cable connected to a computer.

quiz.q: Which real-life technology uses PIR sensors?
quiz.explanation: PIR sensors are widely used in automatic doors, alarms, and smart lighting systems.

quiz.q: What do students learn from this project?
quiz.explanation: Students learn how sensors and automation systems work together in smart technology.
